evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Implement data quality frameworks: tests, freshness checks, anomaly detection, and clear ownership and SLAs on key datasets
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Implementing data quality frameworks including tests, freshness checks, anomaly detection, and clear SLAs is explicit governance/quality language, supporting a rigour classification.
evidence.domain_risk: business KPIs into reliable, self-service data models
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Turning business KPIs into reliable, self-service data models represents business-decision stakes without regulatory or financial-reporting consequence, matching moderate.
evidence.collaboration_width: engineers (event tracking collaboration); Product; CS; Sales
evidence.data_team_maturity: This is our first dedicated analytics hire. You won't inherit a legacy stack or fight bureaucracy—you'll design the analytics function from scratch
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Being described as the company's first dedicated analytics hire with no legacy stack to inherit is a direct statement of building the data function from scratch, matching early maturity.
evidence.jd_authorship: designing and maintaining a dbt project on BigQuery with staging, intermediate, and mart layers
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specifying the exact dbt project structure (staging, intermediate, and mart layers) on BigQuery is concrete technical detail indicating hiring-manager authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Model product analytics events (user interactions, feature adoption, feedback signals), engineering metrics, and business KPIs into reliable, self-serve data models. Support Product teams with ad-hoc analysis: feature impact, user segmentation, satisfaction trends, and power-user behavior.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Modeling product analytics events and supporting Product teams with ad-hoc analysis explicitly centers the role on the product function as primary stakeholder.
evidence.autonomy_level: This is our first dedicated analytics hire. You won't inherit a legacy stack or fight bureaucracy—you'll design the analytics function from scratch with direct executive sponsorship, a modern stack (BigQuery, dbt, Pub/Sub, Python, GCP), and real strategic impact from day one.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Being trusted to design the analytics function from scratch with direct executive sponsorship and real strategic impact from day one is a clear statement of setting direction, matching strategic autonomy.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: you'll design the analytics function from scratch with direct executive sponsorship, a modern stack (BigQuery, dbt, Pub/Sub, Python, GCP)
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Designing the analytics function from scratch with a modern stack and no legacy system to inherit is the clearest possible statement of greenfield work.
evidence.language_gate: Fluent English; Italian is a strong plus
evidence.ai_role: a modern stack (BigQuery, dbt, Pub/Sub, Python, GCP)
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Tech stack mentions traditional data tools only; no AI coding tools, AI-ready infrastructure, or GenAI responsibilities signaled.
evidence.testing_framing: Implement data quality frameworks: tests, freshness checks, anomaly detection, and clear ownership and SLAs on key datasets
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Candidate owns quality practice via 'Implement' verb paired with frameworks, tests, and SLA accountability.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: clear ownership and SLAs on key datasets; freshness checks, anomaly detection
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Operational reliability framed through SLAs and data correctness checks, not compliance or stakeholder-trust dominance.
